title: authsmith configuration
description: Compile-time config consumed by authsmith::generate!(). SMTP credentials and DB connection strings are NOT part of this file — those stay in runtime env vars.

http.description: Controls whether/how HTTP endpoints are generated. Omit entirely if you only want the framework-agnostic handler functions and will wire routes yourself.

automatically_generate_endpoints.description: If true, generate ready-to-mount routes for the chosen framework. If false, only the underlying handler functions/services are generated.

base_path.description: Renamed from `endpoint_hase_path` in the original draft — assumed to be a typo for `base_path`.

framework.description: Required whenever automatically_generate_endpoints is true (the default). No default value on purpose — pick explicitly rather than guessing your stack. Use `none` if you only want handler functions. Not structurally enforced here (kept out of the schema for validator compatibility) — authsmith::generate!() will refuse to compile without it in that case.

email.description: Feature toggles for transactional auth emails. SMTP transport/creds are supplied at runtime, not here.

registration.description: Send a verification email on registration.

forgot_password.description: Enable the forgot-password / reset-password email flow.

required.description: Force MFA enrollment for all users.

max_trusted_devices.description: Renamed from `max_list_of_authorized_connections`. ASSUMPTION: this is the cap on remembered/trusted devices that can skip MFA after initial verification — confirm this matches your intent, the original name was ambiguous. If an integer, must be >= 1. The only valid string value is "*" (unlimited).

id_strategy.description: Primary key strategy for the users table. uuidv4/ulid avoid ID-enumeration attacks directly, which is the actual fix for the concern hash_user_ids was gesturing at.

hash_user_ids.description: Store an additional opaque hashed handle for externally-exposed user references (URLs, tokens), layered on top of id_strategy. Extra obfuscation, not a substitute for id_strategy.

allowed_domains.description: Replaces the old `allowed_providers: ["*", "gmail.com"]` — that mixed a wildcard and a specific value in one array with no defined precedence. `domains` is required when mode isn't `allow_all` (not structurally enforced here for validator compatibility; authsmith::generate!() checks this at compile time).

check_breached_passwords.description: Reject passwords found via the HaveIBeenPwned k-anonymity API. NIST 800-63B recommends this over composition rules; the requires_* flags above are kept for teams with compliance requirements that still mandate them.

additional_fields.description: Arbitrary extra columns on the user record. Keys are field names.
